name: Pickled Eggs in Pickled Beet Juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Select small, young beets. Wash them.
2. Leave 3 inches of tops on and roots intact.
3. Cook until skins slip easily (about 15 minutes).
4. Pack beets into jars within 1/2 inch of the top.
5. Pour boiling liquid syrup over beets to within 1/2 inch of the top of the jar.
6. Process in a boiling water bath for 30 minutes.
7. After about 2 months, when the beets have cured, eat half of them.
8. Drop hard-boiled eggs into the pickled beet juice and refrigerate for about 2 weeks.
9. Alternatively, buy a large jar of pickled beets, eat half, drop in hard-boiled eggs, refrigerate, and let sit for a couple of weeks until the beet juice penetrates.
